specification.description:
DATA-MODEL: globe-viewer-state FIELDS:
- longitude: <class 'float'>
- longitude_note: <class 'str'>
- latitude: <class 'float'>
- latitude_note: <class 'str'>
- altitude_m: <class 'float'>
- altitude_m_note: <class 'str'>
- base_map: <class 'str'>
specification.notes: A data class to specify what data fields will be stored for remembering cookied user's current state within the app, during and between sessions.

context.altitude_m_note: The user's portal altitude from earth mean surface level
context.latitude_note: The user's view portal's center point latitude in decimal form
context.longitude_note: The user's view portal's center point longitude in decimal form

specification.description:
Feature Specification: OnFirstVisit
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.
specification.notes:
1. When a user visits for the very first time, generate a cookie called GLOBAL_VIEWER_UUID (containing a UUID) to know that they've visited. Generate a second cookie named GLOBAL_VIEWER_STATE to contain GlobalViewerPortalState's values, stored as JSON that's been URI-encoded; LLM will provide code that performs the: encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(GlobalViewerPortalState instance)) and cookie decoding e.g. JSON.parse(decodeURIComponent(cookie value)).
2. Perform the steps in OnVisit(Feature) to intialize the portal display.

specification.description:
Feature Specification: OnReturnVisit
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.
specification.notes: 1. Check for GLOBAL_VIEWER_UUID, and if GLOBAL_VIEWER_STATE restore its encoded values to the GlobalViewerPortalState instance. 2. Perform the steps in OnVisit(Feature) to intialize the portal display

specification.description:
Feature Specification: OnVisit
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.
specification.notes: When a user opens the site in a web browser, perform all software tasks necessary to load MapLibre GL JS's libraries, set the map/globe view portal up, initialize the portal to the default longitude, latitude and altitude

specification.description:
Feature Specification: OnVisitTermination
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.
specification.notes: 1. Ensure that when the current browser tab or window closes, that the current GlobalViewerPortalState values are written to GLOBAL_VIEWER_STATE one last time. 2. Then perform all steps necessary to shut down MapLibre GL JS's currently running code, unload allocated objects -whatever's recommended by that project.

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: SingleFile REQUIREMENT-ID: spec.global_viewer_portal.SingleFile
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.
specification.notes:
Instruction: Generate all the source in a single HTML file, including ECMAscript"
Simplify this project at its early stages, while still evaluating libspec and MapLibre GL JS

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: TargetCompatibleECMAScript REQUIREMENT-ID: spec.global_viewer_portal.TargetCompatibleECMAScript
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.
specification.notes:
The LLM should generate code compatible with modern browsers, and use Babel & core-js to ensure as wide of an audience as possible via transpiling and/or polyfilling
Ensure this web app runs on as many devices as possible

specification.description:
Requirement TITLE: TargetMapLibreGLJS REQUIREMENT-ID: spec.global_viewer_portal.TargetMapLibreGLJS
Insert REQUIREMENT-ID into any source code for cross reference purposes.
It's important that error handling be done excellently. If a function can fail, then it needs to do so in the most elegant way possble. Error reporting, handling, exceptions and all aspects of failure must be taken to extreme. It should be possible to understand the program by reading the error messages.
specification.notes:
Make use of MapeLibre GL JS open-source library
- provides core map and globe visualization software and tile data - provides a software base that handles an estimated 99% of this app's needs"
